info.name: Mark
info.desc: The Holy Gospel According to Mark

1:
This is the Good News about Jesus the Messiah, the Son of God.
 It began
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
1:1 Some manuscripts do not include the Son of God.
2:
just as the prophet Isaiah had written:
“Look, I am sending my messenger ahead of you,
and he will prepare your way.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
3:
He is a voice shouting in the wilderness,
‘Prepare the way for the 
Lord
’s coming!
Clear the road for him!’
”
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
; 1:3 Isa 40:3 (Greek version).
4: This messenger was John the Baptist. He was in the wilderness and preached that people should be baptized to show that they had repented of their sins and turned to God to be forgiven.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
5: All of Judea, including all the people of Jerusalem, went out to see and hear John. And when they confessed their sins, he baptized them in the Jordan River.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
6: His clothes were woven from coarse camel hair, and he wore a leather belt around his waist. For food he ate locusts and wild honey.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
7: John announced: “Someone is coming soon who is greater than I am—so much greater that I’m not even worthy to stoop down like a slave and untie the straps of his sandals.
John the Baptist Prepares the Way
8:
I baptize you with
 water, but he will baptize you with the Holy Spirit!”
The Baptism and Temptation of Jesus
1:8 Or in; also in 1:8b.
9: One day Jesus came from Nazareth in Galilee, and John baptized him in the Jordan River.
The Baptism and Temptation of Jesus
10:
As Jesus came up out of the water, he saw the heavens splitting apart and the Holy Spirit descending on him
 like a dove.
The Baptism and Temptation of Jesus
1:10 Or toward him, or into him.
11: And a voice from heaven said, “You are my dearly loved Son, and you bring me great joy.”
The Baptism and Temptation of Jesus
12: The Spirit then compelled Jesus to go into the wilderness,
The Baptism and Temptation of Jesus
13: where he was tempted by Satan for forty days. He was out among the wild animals, and angels took care of him.
The Baptism and Temptation of Jesus
14: Later on, after John was arrested, Jesus went into Galilee, where he preached God’s Good News.
The Baptism and Temptation of Jesus
1:14 Some manuscripts read the Good News of the Kingdom of God.
15: “The time promised by God has come at last!” he announced. “The Kingdom of God is near! Repent of your sins and believe the Good News!”
The First Disciples
16:
One day as Jesus was walking along the shore of the Sea of Galilee, he saw Simon
 and his brother Andrew throwing a net into the water, for they fished for a living.
The First Disciples
1:16 Simon is called “Peter” in 3:16 and thereafter.
17: Jesus called out to them, “Come, follow me, and I will show you how to fish for people!”
The First Disciples
18: And they left their nets at once and followed him.
The First Disciples
19: A little farther up the shore Jesus saw Zebedee’s sons, James and John, in a boat repairing their nets.
The First Disciples
20: He called them at once, and they also followed him, leaving their father, Zebedee, in the boat with the hired men.
Jesus Casts Out an Evil Spirit
21: Jesus and his companions went to the town of Capernaum. When the Sabbath day came, he went into the synagogue and began to teach.
Jesus Casts Out an Evil Spirit
22: The people were amazed at his teaching, for he taught with real authority—quite unlike the teachers of religious law.
Jesus Casts Out an Evil Spirit
23:
Suddenly, a man in the synagogue who was possessed by an evil
 spirit cried out,
Jesus Casts Out an Evil Spirit
1:23 Greek unclean; also in 1:26, 27.
24: “Why are you interfering with us, Jesus of Nazareth? Have you come to destroy us? I know who you are—the Holy One of God!”
Jesus Casts Out an Evil Spirit
25: But Jesus reprimanded him. “Be quiet! Come out of the man,” he ordered.
Jesus Casts Out an Evil Spirit
26: At that, the evil spirit screamed, threw the man into a convulsion, and then came out of him.
Jesus Casts Out an Evil Spirit
27: Amazement gripped the audience, and they began to discuss what had happened. “What sort of new teaching is this?” they asked excitedly. “It has such authority! Even evil spirits obey his orders!”
Jesus Casts Out an Evil Spirit
28: The news about Jesus spread quickly throughout the entire region of Galilee.
Jesus Heals Many People
29: After Jesus left the synagogue with James and John, they went to Simon and Andrew’s home.
Jesus Heals Many People
30: Now Simon’s mother-in-law was sick in bed with a high fever. They told Jesus about her right away.
Jesus Heals Many People
31: So he went to her bedside, took her by the hand, and helped her sit up. Then the fever left her, and she prepared a meal for them.
Jesus Heals Many People
32: That evening after sunset, many sick and demon-possessed people were brought to Jesus.
Jesus Heals Many People
33: The whole town gathered at the door to watch.
Jesus Heals Many People
34: So Jesus healed many people who were sick with various diseases, and he cast out many demons. But because the demons knew who he was, he did not allow them to speak.
Jesus Preaches in Galilee
35: Before daybreak the next morning, Jesus got up and went out to an isolated place to pray.
Jesus Preaches in Galilee
36: Later Simon and the others went out to find him.
37: When they found him, they said, “Everyone is looking for you.”
Jesus Preaches in Galilee
38: But Jesus replied, “We must go on to other towns as well, and I will preach to them, too. That is why I came.”
Jesus Preaches in Galilee
39: So he traveled throughout the region of Galilee, preaching in the synagogues and casting out demons.
Jesus Heals a Man with Leprosy
40: A man with leprosy came and knelt in front of Jesus, begging to be healed. “If you are willing, you can heal me and make me clean,” he said.
Jesus Heals a Man with Leprosy
41: Moved with compassion, Jesus reached out and touched him. “I am willing,” he said. “Be healed!”
Jesus Heals a Man with Leprosy
1:41 Some manuscripts read Moved with anger.
42: Instantly the leprosy disappeared, and the man was healed.
Jesus Heals a Man with Leprosy
43: Then Jesus sent him on his way with a stern warning:
Jesus Heals a Man with Leprosy
44: “Don’t tell anyone about this. Instead, go to the priest and let him examine you. Take along the offering required in the law of Moses for those who have been healed of leprosy. This will be a public testimony that you have been cleansed.”
Jesus Heals a Man with Leprosy
45: But the man went and spread the word, proclaiming to everyone what had happened. As a result, large crowds soon surrounded Jesus, and he couldn’t publicly enter a town anywhere. He had to stay out in the secluded places, but people from everywhere kept coming to him.
Jesus Heals a Man with Leprosy

1: When Jesus returned to Capernaum several days later, the news spread quickly that he was back home.
Jesus Heals a Paralyzed Man
2: Soon the house where he was staying was so packed with visitors that there was no more room, even outside the door. While he was preaching God’s word to them,
Jesus Heals a Paralyzed Man
3: four men arrived carrying a paralyzed man on a mat.
Jesus Heals a Paralyzed Man
4: They couldn’t bring him to Jesus because of the crowd, so they dug a hole through the roof above his head. Then they lowered the man on his mat, right down in front of Jesus.
Jesus Heals a Paralyzed Man
5: Seeing their faith, Jesus said to the paralyzed man, “My child, your sins are forgiven.”
Jesus Heals a Paralyzed Man
6: But some of the teachers of religious law who were sitting there thought to themselves,
Jesus Heals a Paralyzed Man
7: “What is he saying? This is blasphemy! Only God can forgive sins!”
Jesus Heals a Paralyzed Man
8: Jesus knew immediately what they were thinking, so he asked them, “Why do you question this in your hearts?
Jesus Heals a Paralyzed Man
9: Is it easier to say to the paralyzed man ‘Your sins are forgiven,’ or ‘Stand up, pick up your mat, and walk’?
Jesus Heals a Paralyzed Man
10: So I will prove to you that the Son of Man has the authority on earth to forgive sins.” Then Jesus turned to the paralyzed man and said,
Jesus Heals a Paralyzed Man
2:10 “Son of Man” is a title Jesus used for himself.
11: “Stand up, pick up your mat, and go home!”
Jesus Heals a Paralyzed Man
12: And the man jumped up, grabbed his mat, and walked out through the stunned onlookers. They were all amazed and praised God, exclaiming, “We’ve never seen anything like this before!”
Jesus Calls Levi (Matthew)
13: Then Jesus went out to the lakeshore again and taught the crowds that were coming to him.
Jesus Calls Levi (Matthew)
14: As he walked along, he saw Levi son of Alphaeus sitting at his tax collector’s booth. “Follow me and be my disciple,” Jesus said to him. So Levi got up and followed him.
Jesus Calls Levi (Matthew)
15: Later, Levi invited Jesus and his disciples to his home as dinner guests, along with many tax collectors and other disreputable sinners. (There were many people of this kind among Jesus’ followers.)
Jesus Calls Levi (Matthew)
16:
But when the teachers of religious law who were Pharisees
 saw him eating with tax collectors and other sinners, they asked his disciples, “Why does he eat with such scum?
”
Jesus Calls Levi (Matthew)
2:16a Greek the scribes of the Pharisees.; 2:16b Greek with tax collectors and sinners?
17: When Jesus heard this, he told them, “Healthy people don’t need a doctor—sick people do. I have come to call not those who think they are righteous, but those who know they are sinners.”
A Discussion about Fasting
18: Once when John’s disciples and the Pharisees were fasting, some people came to Jesus and asked, “Why don’t your disciples fast like John’s disciples and the Pharisees do?”
A Discussion about Fasting
19: Jesus replied, “Do wedding guests fast while celebrating with the groom? Of course not. They can’t fast while the groom is with them.
A Discussion about Fasting
20: But someday the groom will be taken away from them, and then they will fast.
A Discussion about Fasting
21: “Besides, who would patch old clothing with new cloth? For the new patch would shrink and rip away from the old cloth, leaving an even bigger tear than before.
A Discussion about Fasting
22: “And no one puts new wine into old wineskins. For the wine would burst the wineskins, and the wine and the skins would both be lost. New wine calls for new wineskins.”
A Discussion about the Sabbath
23: One Sabbath day as Jesus was walking through some grainfields, his disciples began breaking off heads of grain to eat.
A Discussion about the Sabbath
24: But the Pharisees said to Jesus, “Look, why are they breaking the law by harvesting grain on the Sabbath?”
A Discussion about the Sabbath
25: Jesus said to them, “Haven’t you ever read in the Scriptures what David did when he and his companions were hungry?
A Discussion about the Sabbath
26: He went into the house of God (during the days when Abiathar was high priest) and broke the law by eating the sacred loaves of bread that only the priests are allowed to eat. He also gave some to his companions.”
A Discussion about the Sabbath
27: Then Jesus said to them, “The Sabbath was made to meet the needs of people, and not people to meet the requirements of the Sabbath.
A Discussion about the Sabbath
28: So the Son of Man is Lord, even over the Sabbath!”
A Discussion about the Sabbath

1: Jesus went into the synagogue again and noticed a man with a deformed hand.
Jesus Heals on the Sabbath
2: Since it was the Sabbath, Jesus’ enemies watched him closely. If he healed the man’s hand, they planned to accuse him of working on the Sabbath.
Jesus Heals on the Sabbath
3: Jesus said to the man with the deformed hand, “Come and stand in front of everyone.”
Jesus Heals on the Sabbath
4: Then he turned to his critics and asked, “Does the law permit good deeds on the Sabbath, or is it a day for doing evil? Is this a day to save life or to destroy it?” But they wouldn’t answer him.
Jesus Heals on the Sabbath
5: He looked around at them angrily and was deeply saddened by their hard hearts. Then he said to the man, “Hold out your hand.” So the man held out his hand, and it was restored!
Jesus Heals on the Sabbath
6: At once the Pharisees went away and met with the supporters of Herod to plot how to kill Jesus.
Crowds Follow Jesus
7: Jesus went out to the lake with his disciples, and a large crowd followed him. They came from all over Galilee, Judea,
Crowds Follow Jesus
8: Jerusalem, Idumea, from east of the Jordan River, and even from as far north as Tyre and Sidon. The news about his miracles had spread far and wide, and vast numbers of people came to see him.
Crowds Follow Jesus
9: Jesus instructed his disciples to have a boat ready so the crowd would not crush him.
Crowds Follow Jesus
10: He had healed many people that day, so all the sick people eagerly pushed forward to touch him.
Crowds Follow Jesus
11:
And whenever those possessed by evil
 spirits caught sight of him, the spirits would throw them to the ground in front of him shrieking, “You are the Son of God!”
Crowds Follow Jesus
3:11 Greek unclean; also in 3:30.
12: But Jesus sternly commanded the spirits not to reveal who he was.
Jesus Chooses the Twelve Apostles
13: Afterward Jesus went up on a mountain and called out the ones he wanted to go with him. And they came to him.
Jesus Chooses the Twelve Apostles
14:
Then he appointed twelve of them and called them his apostles.
 They were to accompany him, and he would send them out to preach,
Jesus Chooses the Twelve Apostles
3:14 Some manuscripts do not include and called them his apostles.
15: giving them authority to cast out demons.
Jesus Chooses the Twelve Apostles
16:
These are the twelve he chose:
Simon (whom he named Peter),
Jesus Chooses the Twelve Apostles
17:
James and John (the sons of Zebedee, but Jesus nicknamed them “Sons of Thunder”
),
Jesus Chooses the Twelve Apostles
3:17 Greek whom he named Boanerges, which means Sons of Thunder.
18:
Andrew,
Philip,
Bartholomew,
Matthew,
Thomas,
James (son of Alphaeus),
Thaddaeus,
Simon (the zealot
),
Jesus Chooses the Twelve Apostles
; 3:18 Greek the Cananean, an Aramaic term for Jewish nationalists.
19: Judas Iscariot (who later betrayed him).
Jesus and the Prince of Demons
20: One time Jesus entered a house, and the crowds began to gather again. Soon he and his disciples couldn’t even find time to eat.
Jesus and the Prince of Demons
21: When his family heard what was happening, they tried to take him away. “He’s out of his mind,” they said.
Jesus and the Prince of Demons
22:
But the teachers of religious law who had arrived from Jerusalem said, “He’s possessed by Satan,
 the prince of demons. That’s where he gets the power to cast out demons.”
Jesus and the Prince of Demons
3:22 Greek Beelzeboul; other manuscripts read Beezeboul; Latin version reads Beelzebub.
23: Jesus called them over and responded with an illustration. “How can Satan cast out Satan?” he asked.
Jesus and the Prince of Demons
24: “A kingdom divided by civil war will collapse.
Jesus and the Prince of Demons
25: Similarly, a family splintered by feuding will fall apart.
Jesus and the Prince of Demons
26: And if Satan is divided and fights against himself, how can he stand? He would never survive.
Jesus and the Prince of Demons
27: Let me illustrate this further. Who is powerful enough to enter the house of a strong man and plunder his goods? Only someone even stronger—someone who could tie him up and then plunder his house.
Jesus and the Prince of Demons
28: “I tell you the truth, all sin and blasphemy can be forgiven,
Jesus and the Prince of Demons
29: but anyone who blasphemes the Holy Spirit will never be forgiven. This is a sin with eternal consequences.”
Jesus and the Prince of Demons
30: He told them this because they were saying, “He’s possessed by an evil spirit.”
The True Family of Jesus
31: Then Jesus’ mother and brothers came to see him. They stood outside and sent word for him to come out and talk with them.
The True Family of Jesus
32:
There was a crowd sitting around Jesus, and someone said, “Your mother and your brothers
 are outside asking for you.”
The True Family of Jesus
3:32 Some manuscripts add and sisters.
33: Jesus replied, “Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?”
The True Family of Jesus
34: Then he looked at those around him and said, “Look, these are my mother and brothers.
The True Family of Jesus
35: Anyone who does God’s will is my brother and sister and mother.”
The True Family of Jesus

1: Once again Jesus began teaching by the lakeshore. A very large crowd soon gathered around him, so he got into a boat. Then he sat in the boat while all the people remained on the shore.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
2: He taught them by telling many stories in the form of parables, such as this one:
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
3: “Listen! A farmer went out to plant some seed.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
4: As he scattered it across his field, some of the seed fell on a footpath, and the birds came and ate it.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
5: Other seed fell on shallow soil with underlying rock. The seed sprouted quickly because the soil was shallow.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
6: But the plant soon wilted under the hot sun, and since it didn’t have deep roots, it died.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
7: Other seed fell among thorns that grew up and choked out the tender plants so they produced no grain.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
8: Still other seeds fell on fertile soil, and they sprouted, grew, and produced a crop that was thirty, sixty, and even a hundred times as much as had been planted!”
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
9: Then he said, “Anyone with ears to hear should listen and understand.”
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
10: Later, when Jesus was alone with the twelve disciples and with the others who were gathered around, they asked him what the parables meant.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
11: He replied, “You are permitted to understand the secret of the Kingdom of God. But I use parables for everything I say to outsiders,
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
4:11 Greek mystery.
12: so that the Scriptures might be fulfilled: ‘When they see what I do, they will learn nothing. When they hear what I say, they will not understand. Otherwise, they will turn to me and be forgiven.’ ”
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
; 4:12  Isa 6:9-10 (Greek version).
13: Then Jesus said to them, “If you can’t understand the meaning of this parable, how will you understand all the other parables?
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
14: The farmer plants seed by taking God’s word to others.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
15: The seed that fell on the footpath represents those who hear the message, only to have Satan come at once and take it away.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
16: The seed on the rocky soil represents those who hear the message and immediately receive it with joy.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
17: But since they don’t have deep roots, they don’t last long. They fall away as soon as they have problems or are persecuted for believing God’s word.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
18: The seed that fell among the thorns represents others who hear God’s word,
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
19: but all too quickly the message is crowded out by the worries of this life, the lure of wealth, and the desire for other things, so no fruit is produced.
Parable of the Farmer Scattering Seed
20: And the seed that fell on good soil represents those who hear and accept God’s word and produce a harvest of thirty, sixty, or even a hundred times as much as had been planted!”
Parable of the Lamp
21: Then Jesus asked them, “Would anyone light a lamp and then put it under a basket or under a bed? Of course not! A lamp is placed on a stand, where its light will shine.
Parable of the Lamp
22: For everything that is hidden will eventually be brought into the open, and every secret will be brought to light.
Parable of the Lamp
23: Anyone with ears to hear should listen and understand.”
Parable of the Lamp
24: Then he added, “Pay close attention to what you hear. The closer you listen, the more understanding you will be given —and you will receive even more.
Parable of the Lamp
4:24 Or The measure you give will be the measure you get back.
25: To those who listen to my teaching, more understanding will be given. But for those who are not listening, even what little understanding they have will be taken away from them.”
Parable of the Growing Seed
26: Jesus also said, “The Kingdom of God is like a farmer who scatters seed on the ground.
Parable of the Growing Seed
27: Night and day, while he’s asleep or awake, the seed sprouts and grows, but he does not understand how it happens.
Parable of the Growing Seed
28: The earth produces the crops on its own. First a leaf blade pushes through, then the heads of wheat are formed, and finally the grain ripens.
Parable of the Growing Seed
29: And as soon as the grain is ready, the farmer comes and harvests it with a sickle, for the harvest time has come.”
Parable of the Mustard Seed
30: Jesus said, “How can I describe the Kingdom of God? What story should I use to illustrate it?
Parable of the Mustard Seed
31: It is like a mustard seed planted in the ground. It is the smallest of all seeds,
Parable of the Mustard Seed
32: but it becomes the largest of all garden plants; it grows long branches, and birds can make nests in its shade.”
Parable of the Mustard Seed
33: Jesus used many similar stories and illustrations to teach the people as much as they could understand.
Parable of the Mustard Seed
34: In fact, in his public ministry he never taught without using parables; but afterward, when he was alone with his disciples, he explained everything to them.
Jesus Calms the Storm
35: As evening came, Jesus said to his disciples, “Let’s cross to the other side of the lake.”
Jesus Calms the Storm
36: So they took Jesus in the boat and started out, leaving the crowds behind (although other boats followed).
37: But soon a fierce storm came up. High waves were breaking into the boat, and it began to fill with water.
Jesus Calms the Storm
38: Jesus was sleeping at the back of the boat with his head on a cushion. The disciples woke him up, shouting, “Teacher, don’t you care that we’re going to drown?”
Jesus Calms the Storm
39: When Jesus woke up, he rebuked the wind and said to the waves, “Silence! Be still!” Suddenly the wind stopped, and there was a great calm.
40: Then he asked them, “Why are you afraid? Do you still have no faith?”
41: The disciples were absolutely terrified. “Who is this man?” they asked each other. “Even the wind and waves obey him!”

1: So they arrived at the other side of the lake, in the region of the Gerasenes.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
5:1 Other manuscripts read Gadarenes; still others read Gergesenes. See Matt 8:28; Luke 8:26.
2:
When Jesus climbed out of the boat, a man possessed by an evil
 spirit came out from the tombs to meet him.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
5:2 Greek unclean; also in 5:8, 13.
3: This man lived in the burial caves and could no longer be restrained, even with a chain.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
4: Whenever he was put into chains and shackles—as he often was—he snapped the chains from his wrists and smashed the shackles. No one was strong enough to subdue him.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
5: Day and night he wandered among the burial caves and in the hills, howling and cutting himself with sharp stones.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
6: When Jesus was still some distance away, the man saw him, ran to meet him, and bowed low before him.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
7: With a shriek, he screamed, “Why are you interfering with me, Jesus, Son of the Most High God? In the name of God, I beg you, don’t torture me!”
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
8: For Jesus had already said to the spirit, “Come out of the man, you evil spirit.”
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
9: Then Jesus demanded, “What is your name?” And he replied, “My name is Legion, because there are many of us inside this man.”
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
10: Then the evil spirits begged him again and again not to send them to some distant place.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
11: There happened to be a large herd of pigs feeding on the hillside nearby.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
12: “Send us into those pigs,” the spirits begged. “Let us enter them.”
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
13: So Jesus gave them permission. The evil spirits came out of the man and entered the pigs, and the entire herd of about 2,000 pigs plunged down the steep hillside into the lake and drowned in the water.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
14: The herdsmen fled to the nearby town and the surrounding countryside, spreading the news as they ran. People rushed out to see what had happened.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
15: A crowd soon gathered around Jesus, and they saw the man who had been possessed by the legion of demons. He was sitting there fully clothed and perfectly sane, and they were all afraid.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
16: Then those who had seen what happened told the others about the demon-possessed man and the pigs.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
17: And the crowd began pleading with Jesus to go away and leave them alone.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
18: As Jesus was getting into the boat, the man who had been demon possessed begged to go with him.
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
19: But Jesus said, “No, go home to your family, and tell them everything the Lord has done for you and how merciful he has been.”
Jesus Heals a Demon-Possessed Man
20:
So the man started off to visit the Ten Towns
 of that region and began to proclaim the great things Jesus had done for him; and everyone was amazed at what he told them.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
5:20 Greek Decapolis.
21: Jesus got into the boat again and went back to the other side of the lake, where a large crowd gathered around him on the shore.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
22: Then a leader of the local synagogue, whose name was Jairus, arrived. When he saw Jesus, he fell at his feet,
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
23: pleading fervently with him. “My little daughter is dying,” he said. “Please come and lay your hands on her; heal her so she can live.”
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
24: Jesus went with him, and all the people followed, crowding around him.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
25: A woman in the crowd had suffered for twelve years with constant bleeding.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
26: She had suffered a great deal from many doctors, and over the years she had spent everything she had to pay them, but she had gotten no better. In fact, she had gotten worse.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
27: She had heard about Jesus, so she came up behind him through the crowd and touched his robe.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
28: For she thought to herself, “If I can just touch his robe, I will be healed.”
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
29: Immediately the bleeding stopped, and she could feel in her body that she had been healed of her terrible condition.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
30: Jesus realized at once that healing power had gone out from him, so he turned around in the crowd and asked, “Who touched my robe?”
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
31: His disciples said to him, “Look at this crowd pressing around you. How can you ask, ‘Who touched me?’”
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
32: But he kept on looking around to see who had done it.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
33: Then the frightened woman, trembling at the realization of what had happened to her, came and fell to her knees in front of him and told him what she had done.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
34: And he said to her, “Daughter, your faith has made you well. Go in peace. Your suffering is over.”
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
35: While he was still speaking to her, messengers arrived from the home of Jairus, the leader of the synagogue. They told him, “Your daughter is dead. There’s no use troubling the Teacher now.”
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
36: But Jesus overheard them and said to Jairus, “Don’t be afraid. Just have faith.”
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
37: Then Jesus stopped the crowd and wouldn’t let anyone go with him except Peter, James, and John (the brother of James).
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
38: When they came to the home of the synagogue leader, Jesus saw much commotion and weeping and wailing.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
39: He went inside and asked, “Why all this commotion and weeping? The child isn’t dead; she’s only asleep.”
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
40: The crowd laughed at him. But he made them all leave, and he took the girl’s father and mother and his three disciples into the room where the girl was lying.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
41: Holding her hand, he said to her, “Talitha koum,” which means “Little girl, get up!”
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
42: And the girl, who was twelve years old, immediately stood up and walked around! They were overwhelmed and totally amazed.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith
43: Jesus gave them strict orders not to tell anyone what had happened, and then he told them to give her something to eat.
Jesus Heals in Response to Faith

1: Jesus left that part of the country and returned with his disciples to Nazareth, his hometown.
Jesus Rejected at Nazareth
2: The next Sabbath he began teaching in the synagogue, and many who heard him were amazed. They asked, “Where did he get all this wisdom and the power to perform such miracles?”
